request.question:
Conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles will increase intracellular drug accumulation in HeLa cells by at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin, due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells, while reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40%.
In plain English: can we deliver chemotherapy more precisely to cancer cells by attaching it to a targeting molecule?

hypothesis.intervention: conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles
hypothesis.threshold: at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are specific and present, making the hypothesis CRO-ready.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: doxorubicin folate PLGA nanoparticles HeLa cells drug accumulation
hypothesis.original_hypothesis:
Conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles will increase intracellular drug accumulation in HeLa cells by at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin, due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells, while reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40%.
In plain English: can we deliver chemotherapy more precisely to cancer cells by attaching it to a targeting molecule?

references.title: Hybrid PEGylated chitosan/PLGA nanoparticles designed as pH-responsive vehicles to promote intracellular drug delivery and cancer chemotherapy.
references.relevance_note: This paper discusses PLGA nanoparticles for doxorubicin delivery, which is closely related to the hypothesis, focusing on enhancing drug delivery within cancer cells.

references.title: Transferrin-conjugated doxorubicin-loaded PLGA nanoparticles with pH-responsive behavior: a synergistic approach for cancer therapy
references.relevance_note: Investigates transferrin-conjugated PLGA nanoparticles for doxorubicin, offers insights into PLGA modifications relevant to the proposed hypothesis.

references.title: Combinatorial delivery of superparamagnetic iron oxide nanoparticles (γFe2O3) and doxorubicin using folate conjugated redox sensitive multiblock polymeric nanocarriers for enhancing the chemotherapeutic efficacy in cancer cells.
references.relevance_note: Explores the use of folate-conjugated nanocarriers for doxorubicin delivery in cancer cells, relevant due to the utilization of folate in targeting.

literature_qc.explanation: The intervention of conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles has similarities with the work described in paper [5], which also utilizes folate conjugated nanocarriers for doxorubicin delivery, albeit with a different carrier system. Moreover, papers [3] and [4] also investigate PLGA-based nanoparticles for doxorubicin delivery, indicating that the same biological system (HeLa cells) is being targeted with similar methodologies. However, specifics about intracellular drug accumulation as a measurable outcome might not be shared among these works.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

risks.mitigation: Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.

risks.description: Control strategy may miss process-specific artifacts in handling or reagent preparation.

plan.objective: Determine the intracellular accumulation of doxorubicin delivered via folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles in HeLa cells compared to free doxorubicin.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be treated with conjugated doxorubicin-PLGA nanoparticles and free doxorubicin. The intracellular drug accumulation will be quantified using fluorescence spectrophotometry following cell lysis. An emphasis will be placed on comparing the levels of drug accumulation after a set incubation time.
controls: Free doxorubicin at a concentration of 10 µM in sterile Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium (DMEM).
Untreated HeLa cells in sterile DMEM without doxorubicin.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Dissolve 100 mg of poly(lactic-co-glycolic acid) (PLGA, 50:50, 10 kDa) in 5 mL of dichloromethane (DCM) at room temperature.
Expected outcome: PLGA completely dissolved in DCM.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Add 10 mg of doxorubicin hydrochloride to the PLGA solution and stir for 30 min at room temperature to allow drug solubilization.
Expected outcome: Doxorubicin is solubilized in the PLGA solution.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 45 min | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Emulsify the drug-loaded PLGA solution in 20 mL of 1% (w/v) polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) solution by ultrasonication for 5 min at 100 W using a probe sonicator.
Expected outcome: Stable emulsion of PLGA-doxorubicin formed.

DAY 1 | 2 h | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Quench the emulsion by slowly evaporating DCM at 60°C for 2 h using a rotary evaporator.
Expected outcome: DCM completely evaporated, forming solid nanoparticles.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Nanoparticle Purification
Centrifuge the nanoparticle suspension at 10,000 × g for 30 min at 4°C to pellet the nanoparticles.
Expected outcome: Nanoparticles pelleted, supernatant discarded.

DAY 1 | 10 min | Nanoparticle Re-suspension
Re-suspend the pellet in 1 mL of sterile phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) and sonicate briefly for 1 min.
Expected outcome: Nanoparticles uniformly resuspended in PBS.

DAY 1 | 24 h | Cell Culture Treatment
Seed 1 × 10^5 HeLa cells in a 12-well plate in 1 mL of complete DMEM and allow to adhere for 24 h at 37°C, 5% CO2.
Expected outcome: Cells adhere and proliferate to form a confluent monolayer.

DAY 2 | 4 h | Cell Treatment
Treat the cells with 1 mL of either 10 µM free doxorubicin or 10 µM doxorubicin-conjugated PLGA nanoparticles and incubate for 4 h at 37°C, 5% CO2.
Expected outcome: Cells exposed to treatment for the specified time period.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Cell Lysis and Drug Quantification
Lyse the cells using 200 µL of RIPA buffer and perform fluorescence measurement at 480 nm excitation and 590 nm emission to quantify intracellular doxorubicin accumulation.
Expected outcome: Fluorescence intensity measured, correlating to intracellular doxorubicin concentration.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Analyze fluorescence data using GraphPad Prism by comparing fluorescence intensities between free doxorubicin and doxorubicin-conjugated nanoparticles using a t-test.
Expected outcome: Statistically validated comparison of intracellular accumulation between the two treatment groups.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: Folate receptors are overexpressed in HeLa cells facilitating enhanced uptake of folate-conjugated nanoparticles.
Doxorubicin's fluorescence intensity remains stable post-uptake within HeLa cells.
PLGA nanoparticles are successfully internalized and escape endosomal trapping.
decision_criteria: Proceed if intracellular doxorubicin accumulation in nanoparticle-treated cells is at least 3-fold greater than in free doxorubicin treatment group.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Maintain consistent PLGA batch number for nanoparticle synthesis across replicates.
Standardize HeLa cells to less than 10 passages from frozen stocks for each experiment.
Ensure consistent fluorescence quantification settings (excitation/emission wavelengths) across all assays.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: Determine the intracellular accumulation of doxorubicin delivered via folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles in HeLa cells compared to free doxorubicin.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
